title: Household Production
paragraphs: Gross Domestic Product (GDP) is a measure that reflects the overall economy of a country. It represents the total final transaction value of goods and services produced within a country or a place over a specific period. It includes all private and public consumption, government spending, investment, materials in stock, expenses during construction, and expenses and income from imports and exports. In Nepal, this is calculated annually. According to Trading Economics, in 2023, Nepal's GDP was approximately 40 trillion rupees, and its per capita income was 1,399 US dollars.
